headline: Netflix Raises Prices. Streaming Is Going to Keep Getting More Expensive.
summary: Netflix  stock rose Thursday following news that the company once again raised prices for its multiple subscription tiers, another sign for consumers that streaming will continue to get more expensive from here.  Netflix updated its plans page on Thursday.  “We continue offering a range of prices and plans to meet a variety of needs, and as we deliver more value to our members we are updating our prices to enable us to reinvest in quality entertainment and improve their experience,” a Netflix spokesperson said.

headline: Meta Stock Slides After Back-to-Back Courtroom Losses
summary: Shares of Meta Platforms tumbled in Thursday morning trading, after a pair of back-to-back verdicts found the company to blame for the harmful impact of its products on young people.  In one landmark case, a New Mexico jury found the Facebook and Instagram parent liable for failing to protect young people from online dangers including solicitation, sexually explicit content and human trafficking.  Just one day later, California jurors found Meta and Google’s YouTube negligent for operating a product that harmed children and teens and failed to warn about those dangers.

raw.headline: Meta Stock Slides After Back-to-Back Courtroom Losses

In one landmark case, a New Mexico jury found the Facebook and Instagram parent liable for failing to protect young people from online dangers including solicitation, sexually explicit content and human trafficking.  Just one day later, California jurors found Meta and Google’s YouTube negligent for operating a product that harmed children and teens and failed to warn about those dangers.

headline: Why TurboQuant Hammered Memory Stocks—and Why ‘Jevons’ Paradox’ Means the Market Is Wrong
summary: Highflying memory stocks like Micron and SanDisk have been dented this week and it might have something to do with TurboQuant, a compression algorithm detailed by Google in a research paper this week.  Micron Technology  and  Sandisk  both fell more than 3% on Wednesday and were headed for bigger falls in early trading Thursday.  TurboQuant is an algorithm designed to address bottlenecks in the key-value cache, which Google describes as a “digital cheat sheet,” effectively acting as the short-term memory for an artificial-intelligence model.

TurboQuant is an algorithm designed to address bottlenecks in the key-value cache, which Google describes as a “digital cheat sheet,” effectively acting as the short-term memory for an artificial-intelligence model.

headline: Micron, Other Chip Stocks Slump After Google Unveils New Memory Technology
summary: A selloff in memory chip makers extended to Asia and showed signs of continuing in the U.S. after Google’s unveiled technology this week that could reduce the amount of memory needed in AI models.  Google researchers announced on Tuesday a new compression algorithm called TurboQuant, which they say can shrink the amount of key-value cache memory—essentially, short-term memory—that an AI model uses by at least six times, “all with zero accuracy loss.”  Shares in Micron Technology, the largest American maker of memory chips, closed 3.4% Wednesday.

raw.headline: Micron, Other Chip Stocks Slump After Google Unveils New Memory Technology
raw.summary: A selloff in memory chip makers extended to Asia and showed signs of continuing in the U.S. after Google’s unveiled technology this week that could reduce the amount of memory needed in AI models.  Google researchers announced on Tuesday a new compression algorithm called TurboQuant, which they say can shrink the amount of key-value cache memory—essentially, short-term memory—that an AI model uses by at least six times, “all with zero accuracy loss.”  Shares in Micron Technology, the largest American maker of memory chips, closed 3.4% Wednesday.

headline: US jury verdicts against Meta, Google tee up fight over tech liability shield
summary: Jurors in the first two trials in the U.S. from a growing wave of lawsuits targeting social media firms over harm to children have found Meta and Alphabet's Google liable, potentially teeing up an appeals fight that could reshape how U.S. law shields tech companies from lawsuits.  In California, ‌a Los Angeles jury on Wednesday found Meta and Google liable for a young woman’s depression and suicidal thoughts after she said she became addicted to Instagram and YouTube ‌at a young age, ordering them to pay a combined $6 million in damages.  In a separate New Mexico case, jurors on Tuesday ordered Meta to pay $375 million after finding the company misled users about the safety of its products for ​young users and enabled the sexual exploitation of children on its platforms.

raw.headline: US jury verdicts against Meta, Google tee up fight over tech liability shield
raw.summary: Jurors in the first two trials in the U.S. from a growing wave of lawsuits targeting social media firms over harm to children have found Meta and Alphabet's Google liable, potentially teeing up an appeals fight that could reshape how U.S. law shields tech companies from lawsuits.  In California, ‌a Los Angeles jury on Wednesday found Meta and Google liable for a young woman’s depression and suicidal thoughts after she said she became addicted to Instagram and YouTube ‌at a young age, ordering them to pay a combined $6 million in damages.  In a separate New Mexico case, jurors on Tuesday ordered Meta to pay $375 million after finding the company misled users about the safety of its products for ​young users and enabled the sexual exploitation of children on its platforms.

headline: Meta, Google Risk Big Tobacco-Like Fallout After Addiction Trial
